spec_match.rationale: DevTools MCP verified the rendered cards now use actual CSS custom function calls such as --ease(back-out) and --ease(spring), which compute to the expected cubic-bezier values.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP clicked Play all and a per-card Play button, moved the duration range to 1.7s, and observed the cards' moved state, transition duration, and computed easing update.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Some unsupported / error handling is wired up, but several spec edge cases (boundary inputs, alternate modes, error states) are not surfaced.

references.rationale: The see also section now links to the required chromestatus.com/feature/5179721933651968 entry.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate failure and edge-case branches
openQuestions.detail: Add visible branches for the unsupported / unavailable / error / boundary inputs paths. For built-in AI APIs, that means 'downloadable', 'downloading', 'unavailable' availability states; for HTTP features, error responses and CSP blocks; for CSS features, zero / extreme / inherited values. Today only the happy path is reachable from the UI.

summary: DevTools MCP on Chrome 148 verified /v139/css-custom-functions/easing-library/?verify=after-real-function-call: the support probe computes --ease(spring), rendered cards resolve custom function calls to expected timing functions, Play all moves all 9 balls, the slider updates duration to 1.7s, an individual Play button toggles one card, console/network are clean, and /v139/css-custom-functions/conformance/?verify=easing-after-real-function-call reports 10 PASS / 0 FAIL. The remaining open question is the existing edge-case branch coverage gap.
